concerns.text: Tasks T016-T024 implement simulation using synthetic parametric distributions (LogNormal, Beta, t-distribution) with theoretical means. This silently violates FR-001, FR-002, FR-010, and Constitution Principle VII, which mandate using 'full UCI datasets' and the 'true population mean from the full dataset array' as ground truth. The tasks replace the required data source and ground truth definition without explicit staging or spec amendment.

Task T017 implements sampling 'with replacement' from generated distributions. While FR-010 requires sampling with replacement, it specifically applies to 'the UCI dataset'. The task applies this logic to synthetic data, failing to implement the UCI-specific sampling logic required by FR-001 and FR-010.

Task T020 implements coverage checks against 'the theoretical mean of the distribution'. This violates Constitution Principle VII ('Coverage validation is only valid when the true population mean is known from the full dataset array') and FR-002/FR-010 which require the 'full dataset' to represent the population. The task uses a theoretical mean instead of the dataset mean.

The spec defines edge cases for UCI datasets (fewer than 30 rows, missing values, categorical variables). The tasks in Phase 3 (T016-T024) implement synthetic data generation, rendering these specific UCI edge-case handling requirements unimplemented. The tasks do not include logic to handle UCI-specific constraints.

Tasks T027-T031 aggregate coverage rates across 'multiple distributions'. The spec (FR-006, US-2) requires aggregation across 'multiple datasets' (UCI). The tasks shift the aggregation scope from real-world datasets to synthetic distributions, violating the requirement to generalize findings across real-world distributions.
